articles.title: Isabelle/HOL: The proof assistant behind the Nitro Isolation Engine
articles.summary: Isabelle/HOL's balance of expressiveness, automation, and scalability enabled the world's first formally verified cloud hypervisor.

articles.title: Customized Amazon Nova models improve molecular-property prediction in drug discovery
articles.summary: A single, optimized LLM unifies what previously required multiple models and can serve as a reasoning partner for medical chemists.

articles.title: AWS and Hopkins Engineering announce groundbreaking database for AI/ML antibody design
articles.summary: Built in collaboration with the Gray Lab at Johns Hopkins Whiting School of Engineering, the Antibody Developability Benchmark is powered by one of the most diverse antibody datasets in public literature, enabling transparent performance evaluation for AI-guided antibody design.

articles.title: How Amazon uses agentic AI for vulnerability detection at global scale
articles.summary: Amazon’s RuleForge system uses agentic AI to generate production-ready detection rules 336% faster than traditional methods.

articles.title: Verifying and optimizing post-quantum cryptography at Amazon
articles.summary: How automated reasoning reconciles the demands of security, performance, and maintainability.

articles.title: Improving quality and robustness in LLM-based text-to-speech systems
articles.summary: Low-rank adaptation, data augmentation, and chain-of-thought reasoning are among the techniques enabling accent-free polyglot outputs, improved expressiveness, and reliable synthesis.

articles.title: Formally verified AES-XTS: The first AES algorithm to join s2n-bignum
articles.summary: Simplifying and clarifying the assembly code for core operations enabled automated optimization and verification.

articles.title: Optimizing LoRA target module selection for efficient fine tuning
articles.summary: Ablation study clarifies trade-offs between accuracy and efficiency when using low-rank adaptation (LoRA) to fine-tune AI models.

articles.title: How agentic AI helps heal the systems we can’t replace
articles.summary: By learning the idiosyncrasies of accumulated layers of legacy systems, AI agents can preserve institutional knowledge and provide a unified interface to a range of services.

articles.title: Designing AI agents that know when to step back
articles.summary: As AI agents become more autonomous, the key challenge isn't what they can do; it's how to design the human side of the equation.

articles.title: How AI is changing the nature of mathematical research
articles.summary: What machine learning theorists learned using AI agents to generate proofs — and what comes next.

articles.title: Intelligence isn’t about parameter count. It’s about time.
articles.summary: As AI models grow larger, they become less insightful, not more. To ensure that they continue to learn, we need to reduce their inference time.

articles.title: Why a 12-year-old forecasting paper has stood the test of time
articles.summary: Amazon Scholar Aravind Srinivasan coauthored a 2014 paper about forecasting civil unrest in Latin America, which won a test-of-time award at KDD 2025.

articles.title: How academic collaboration delivers real-world security to Amazon customers
articles.summary: An early meeting between Amazon scientists and Stanford researchers led to cvc5, an open-source tool now powering approximately one billion automated-reasoning checks across AWS every day.

articles.title: Amazon Nova AI Challenge returns with Nova Forge access for competing teams
articles.summary: For the first time in an academic competition, students can customize frontier AI models to build trusted software agents

articles.title: Engaging the AI community through building, research, and shared learning
articles.summary: Advancing AI requires more than breakthrough models. It depends on communities of builders and researchers who experiment, test assumptions, and share what they learn. That belief is guiding how Amazon engages developers and academics around Amazon Nova, Amazon’s portfolio of AI offerings including the Nova models, Nova Forge and Nova Act.

articles.title: A decade of NFL Next Gen Stats innovation
articles.summary: Every NFL game generates millions of tracking data points from 22 RFID-equipped players. Seventy-five machine learning models running on AWS process that data in under a second, transforming football into a sport where every movement is measured, modeled, and instantly analyzed.

articles.title: Customizing multiturn AI agents with reinforcement learning
articles.summary: Leveraging existing environment simulators and reward functions based on verifiable ground truth boosts task success rate, even with small models and small training datasets.

articles.title: Fine-tuning vision-language models on memory-constrained devices
articles.summary: A new hybrid optimization approach allows edge devices to fine-tune vision-language models using only forward passes, achieving up to 7% higher accuracy than existing techniques.

articles.title: The unseen work of building reliable AI agents
articles.summary: "Reinforcement learning gyms" train agents on the many low-level tasks that they must chain together to execute customer requests.

articles.title: The 10 most viewed publications of 2025
articles.summary: From foundation model safety frameworks and formal verification at cloud scale to advanced robotics and multimodal AI reasoning, these are the most viewed publications from Amazon scientists and collaborators in 2025.

articles.title: The 10 most viewed blog posts of 2025
articles.summary: From quantum computing breakthroughs and foundation models for robotics to the evolution of Amazon Aurora and advances in agentic AI, these are the posts that captured readers' attention in 2025.

articles.title: Dialogue Boost: How Amazon is using AI to enhance TV and movie dialogue
articles.summary: New audio-processing technology is making entertainment more accessible for millions of viewers.

articles.title: Amazon Nova Forge: "Open training” paradigm that empowers everyone to build their own frontier AI
articles.summary: New service lets customers mix their own data with the data used to train Amazon Nova at each major stage of model development, enabling deep domain understanding while preventing "catastrophic forgetting".

articles.title: AutoGluon assistant: Zero-code AutoML through multiagent collaboration
articles.summary: A multiagent architecture separates data perception, tool knowledge, execution history, and code generation, enabling ML automation that works with messy, real-world inputs.
